channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI suggests that upcoming IPOs by major AI companies may lead to increased token pricing. The piece frames this as a potential "Tokenpocalypse" scenario driven by market dynamics and going-public strategies.

item.title: Amazing Digital Dentures (a failed project)
item.description: Hugging Face: Hugging Face published a retrospective on a project called Amazing Digital Dentures that did not succeed. The post documents the failed project as a case study.

item.title: Social intelligence Arises Between Minds - Psychology Today
item.description: Google News AI: A Psychology Today article discussing how social intelligence emerges from interactions between multiple minds. The piece explores the psychological foundations of social cognition and collaborative intelligence.

item.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Notion experienced a service disruption that affected Anthropic integration and subsequently restored access. Notion's head of product expressed surprise at the volume of social media attention the incident received.

item.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenAI is reportedly building a "super app" and a senior employee has stated that "chat is dead," suggesting a shift away from traditional chat-based interfaces. The statement indicates OpenAI's internal view on the evolution of their product strategy.

item.title: The OnlyFans Economy of American AI
item.description: futurisold: The article presents an analogy between the American AI economy and the OnlyFans model, offering commentary on the structure or dynamics of the AI industry. The piece uses this framing to make a critique or observation about how AI development and monetization operates.

item.title: The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
item.description: tavioto: The return on investment from AI-assisted coding differs depending on whether a founder is bootstrapped or externally funded. Cost-benefit tradeoffs of AI tools appear distinct for founders without venture capital backing.

item.title: Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
item.description: Google News AI: The Economist explores whether artificial intelligence systems may soon exceed human oversight and control. This reflects ongoing debate in the AI industry about alignment, safety, and the trajectory of AI capabilities.

item.title: Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
item.description: predkambrij: A user is calling for Anthropic to release an official Claude Desktop application for Linux. The request highlights a gap in platform support for Claude's desktop offering.

item.title: LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
item.description: poisonfountain: A software engineer expresses concern that LLMs are diminishing career opportunities and value in software engineering. The author seeks advice on how to navigate this labor market shift.

item.title: Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
item.description: simianwords: The author asks whether Claude and ChatGPT implement thinking efforts (tunable low, medium, high, xhigh levels) via separate models or a single model with different parameters. They note that changing effort mid-conversation breaks Claude's cache but not Codex's performance, suggesting different underlying implementations.

item.title: How Twitter Rewired My Brain
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reflects on how Twitter usage has altered his cognitive patterns and neural responses. The video presents a personal perspective on social media's impact on brain function and habits.

item.title: Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
item.description: devenjarvis: Lathe is a Go CLI tool that uses LLMs to generate hands-on, source-backed tutorials for technical topics, then guides learners through typing code locally in a web UI. It integrates with Claude, Cursor, and Codex as LLM backends and includes features like tables of contents, side-notes, exercises, and the ability to verify code compilation.

item.title: Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts - The Guardian
item.description: Google News AI: The Guardian's analysis examines the AI industry boom through charts showing capital investments and projected returns. The piece uses data visualization to explain the scale of spending in AI and the speculative nature of current returns.

item.title: Automated QA and Testing with AI
item.description: Chrisszz: The post discusses the application of AI methods to automated quality assurance and software testing. This represents commentary on an emerging practice area at the intersection of AI and DevOps.

item.title: Efficient and Training-Free Single-Image Diffusion Models
item.description: yorwba: This work presents an efficient approach to single-image diffusion models that operates without training overhead. The method enables diffusion-based generation and manipulation directly on individual images.

item.title: 7 Ways New Engineers Can Flourish in the Age of AI
item.description: jruohonen: The post offers recommendations for early-career engineers to succeed in modern AI-influenced software development. It presents perspectives on how new engineers can adapt and develop skills relevant to the AI industry landscape.

item.title: I design with Claude more than Figma now
item.description: MrBuddyCasino: A designer reports using Claude for design tasks more frequently than Figma, the industry-standard design tool. This reflects a shift in workflow preferences toward AI-assisted design approaches.

item.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream focused on AI topics including coding, agents, and Claude Code. The session will cover questions from the community while combining casual conversation with chess gameplay.

item.title: Tokenomics: Quantifying Where Tokens Are Used in Agentic Software Engineering
item.description: Anon84: Tokenomics presents an analysis framework for understanding and quantifying where tokens are consumed in agentic software engineering systems. The work focuses on measuring token distribution and efficiency across different components of AI-agent workflows.

item.title: Top Trump artificial intelligence adviser to leave the White House - The Washington Post
item.description: Google News AI: A top artificial intelligence adviser in the Trump White House is departing from the administration. The announcement was reported by The Washington Post.

item.title: Why Aren't We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?
item.description: pseudolus: The post questions why the AI industry lacks systematic measurement of human-level impacts from AI systems. It suggests there is a gap between deploying AI widely and understanding its actual effects on people.

item.title: Is Claude Mythos Coming?
item.description: Nate Herk | AI Automation: Nate Herk analyzes a leaked Mythos identifier on Anthropic's API and skeptically predicts that the capability will quietly fold into the next Claude Opus rather than launch as a distinct product. He frames the leak as market hype rather than evidence of imminent public availability and highlights OpenAI's competitive position as a key factor.

item.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenAI has unveiled Lockdown Mode, a feature designed to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks. The feature reduces the likelihood of data exposure, though ChatGPT may remain vulnerable to some prompt injections.

item.title: Computex 2026: Are We Heading for the Agentic PC Era Yet?
item.description: rbanffy: A Computex 2026 commentary questioning whether the personal computing industry is transitioning toward agentic PCs. The piece examines whether hardware and software announcements at the conference signal a shift toward autonomous, agent-driven computing systems on user devices.

item.title: Claude, Teach Me Something
item.description: dannyboland: A hackernews post from dannyboland requesting Claude to teach them something. This reflects curiosity about using Claude for educational purposes and the conversational capabilities of the model.

item.title: Meta confirms 1000s of Instagram accounts were hacked by abusing its AI chatbot
item.description: speckx: Meta confirmed that thousands of Instagram accounts were compromised through an exploit that abused its AI chatbot. The incident highlights a security vulnerability in AI systems deployed at scale on consumer platforms.

item.title: Law Professors Prefer AI over Peer Answers
item.description: davidbarker: Law professors prefer AI-generated answers over answers from their peers. This suggests perceived quality or utility advantages of AI systems in professional legal contexts.

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI previews expectations for Apple's WWDC 2026 conference, highlighting anticipated updates to Siri and Apple Intelligence. The article positions Siri's revamp as a major focus for the upcoming developer conference.

item.title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
item.description: Matthew Berman: Matthew Berman covers Perplexity's new product called Perplexity Computer, which is positioned as a comprehensive AI system. The video is promotional content focused on this tool release.

item.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his position as White House AI advisor. He is reportedly starting a new institution to continue influencing Trump administration AI policy.

item.title: OpenAI and the White House have competing visions for regulating artificial intelligence - Mashable
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI and the White House hold different visions for how artificial intelligence should be regulated. The article covers the debate over regulatory approaches between the two parties.

item.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
item.description: rsn243: Research paper proposing a unification between decision trees and diffusion models through a flows-based framework. The work connects two traditionally separate machine learning paradigms by showing how tree-based structures can be transformed into and from continuous flow representations.

item.title: 24 Agents tasked to build a $1B app
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej demonstrates 24 AI agents working together on building a large-scale application valued at approximately $1 billion. The video explores how multiple autonomous agents can be coordinated to tackle complex software development tasks.

item.title: AI Can't Care
item.description: mooreds: A commentary titled "AI Can't Care" expresses a position on the limitations of AI systems regarding emotional or moral capability. The piece appears to argue that artificial intelligence lacks genuine capacity for care or concern, addressing a fundamental characteristic of AI cognition.

item.title: LLM Research Papers: The 2026 List (January to May)
item.description: Ahead of AI: Ahead of AI published a curated roundup of notable LLM research papers published between January and May 2026. The list aggregates significant research contributions in the large language model field during that period.

item.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: maltalex: S&P 500 index has rejected SpaceX for inclusion and is also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic. The decision affects major AI and aerospace companies' eligibility for major index inclusion.

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space published a brief commentary noting minimal notable activity in the AI industry on a particular day, characterizing it as a period of consolidation or rest.

item.title: micropython-wasm 0.1a2
item.description: Simon Willison: micropython-wasm 0.1a2 release adds a CLI interface to the micropython-wasm project. The tool enables running MicroPython code in WebAssembly sandboxes, with a command-line interface inspired by documentation needs for illustrating usage examples.

item.title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison released micropython-wasm, a sandbox for executing Python code safely using MicroPython compiled to WebAssembly. The package restricts file access, network connectivity, and other potentially harmful operations while enabling plugin-style code execution in Datasette, LLM, and other tools.

item.title: Ask HN: Why is the HN crowd so anti-AI?
item.description: Ekami: The author questions why Hacker News tends to criticize AI-generated code for bugs and technical debt, arguing that execution speed and user outcomes matter more than code elegance. They claim AI-assisted development can ship products 10x faster and iterate on feedback more effectively than manual development.

item.title: OpenAI Help: Lockdown Mode
item.description: Simon Willison: OpenAI has launched Lockdown Mode, a security feature that prevents data exfiltration from prompt injection attacks by restricting outbound network requests. The feature is rolling out to personal and ChatGPT Business accounts, though it comes with tradeoffs on functionality. Lockdown Mode blocks one leg of the "Lethal Trifecta" security problem by eliminating the attacker's exfiltration vector.

item.title: Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause"
item.description: Wes Roth: Wes Roth reports on Anthropic's call for a global AI pause. The content covers AI industry news and developments from major companies including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and NVIDIA.

item.title: AGI is Here. Anthropic Just Proved It.
item.description: Nate Herk | AI Automation: Nate Herk analyzes Anthropic's "When AI Builds Itself" report showing 80% of shipped code is AI-written, arguing this demonstrates AGI has already arrived. The video explores definitions of AGI, internal metrics supporting this claim, three possible scenarios for the future, and why Anthropic is advocating for caution despite building increasingly powerful systems.

item.title: $1,300,000 in TOKENS
item.description: Matthew Berman: Matthew Berman discusses the monetary cost of processing or generating 1.3 million tokens, likely analyzing pricing models or cost implications in large language model usage.

item.title: Trump to meet with artificial intelligence companies on government profit share plan as soon as next week - Politico
item.description: Google News AI: Trump plans to meet with artificial intelligence companies to discuss a government profit share plan. The meeting is scheduled for the following week. This represents policy engagement between government leadership and the AI industry on revenue-sharing arrangements.

item.title: Transformers are inherently succinct
item.description: brandonb: A paper accepted to ICLR 2026 argues that transformers are inherently succinct. The work was selected as one of three outstanding papers at the conference.

item.title: How to Stop Shipping Low-Quality RL Environments (with Examples)
item.description: Latent Space: An article discussing common quality issues in reinforcement learning environments and practices for improving them. The author argues that poorly implemented RL harnesses actively degrade model performance and provides examples of problems to fix.

item.title: Harness engineering: Leveraging Codex in an agent-first world
item.description: pramodbiligiri: The piece discusses harness engineering and the role of Codex, a code generation model, in an emerging landscape where AI agents are becoming primary. It explores how Codex can be leveraged as a foundational tool in agent-first system design.

item.title: Launch HN: General Instinct (YC P26) – Frontier models on edge devices
item.description: guanming0717: General Instinct open-sourced InstinctRazor, a compression and deployment framework that reduces frontier MoE models like Qwen3.5-122B to 48 GiB GGUF format while preserving performance on benchmarks. The approach preserves always-active components and applies aggressive quantization to routed experts, with on-policy distillation to recover capability loss, enabling deployment on edge hardware with constrained VRAM.
